title: Sentinel Shield consumer-validation record
description: One record emitted by a real-consumer validation driver (tests/prod/2NN-*-consumer.sh) for a SINGLE check against a genuine downstream consumer fixture Sentinel Shield does NOT own. Records are line-delimited JSON (one object per line). A record captures WHICH consumer and (for JS) package manager was exercised, WHICH gate/check ran, its status, a STABLE reason_code, and whether the check was proven LIVE (real toolchain executed) or STRUCTURAL (logic/contract asserted without executing the toolchain). The optional 'ecosystem' field (php|node|react) lets records from different stack drivers share this one schema. status=skip is NEVER a pass and MUST carry a reason: it is an explicitly deferred check (e.g. toolchain absent in this runner) that CI must still run. Validated jq-structurally here (jq -e .); ajv-validated in CI.

schema_version.description: Consumer-validation record schema version. Fixed at '1'. Bump only on a breaking change to this record shape.

consumer.description: Consumer fixture the check ran against (e.g. node-service, react-app, pm-variants/npm, php-library-consumer-fixture).

package_manager.description: Authoritative package manager for the consumer, or 'none' when the check is manager-agnostic (e.g. all PHP/Composer checks).

check.description: Stable identifier of the specific check (e.g. pm-authority, lockfile-authority, one-of-test-runner, typecheck-mutation, static-analysis-mutation, rollback-byte-for-byte).

gate.description: The engine gate the check exercises. The JS gates (package-manager..install) and the PHP gates (manifest/static-analysis/style/config) share this closed set so both drivers conform to one schema.

status.description: Outcome. 'pass' = assertion held (including a mutation the gate correctly caught); 'fail' = a real defect or validation failure; 'skip' = could not run (toolchain/network absent) — recorded as a limitation, never counted as a pass.

reason_code.description: Stable machine code for the outcome (e.g. OK, MULTIPLE_AUTHORITATIVE_LOCKFILES, MANAGER_MISMATCH, MISSING_LOCKFILE, INVALID_MANAGER, TS_COMPILE_FAIL, ESLINT_ERROR, TEST_FAIL, LOCKFILE_RESTORED, LIVE_UNAVAILABLE, STATIC_ANALYSIS_TOOL_MISSING, TOOLCHAIN_ABSENT_COMPOSER, TOOLCHAIN_ABSENT_PHP). Skips MUST carry a reason so CI can enumerate deferred work.

mode.description: 'live' = the real toolchain/manager was executed and observed (or is a live check deferred as skip); 'structural' = logic, contract, or fixture shape was asserted without executing the downstream toolchain.

ecosystem.description: Optional stack tag so records from different drivers share this schema. Omitted for stack-agnostic synthetic fixtures (e.g. package-manager negative cases).

detail.description: Optional human-readable detail (paths, commands, observed values).

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC time the record was emitted.
